content.title: Orthopaedic Surgery
content.definition: The surgical and non-surgical diagnosis, treatment, and rehabilitation of disorders affecting the musculoskeletal system including bones, joints, ligaments, tendons, muscles, and nerves.
content.summary: Orthopaedic surgery encompasses traumatology, reconstructive joint surgery, spine surgery, sports medicine, paediatric orthopaedics, oncology, and hand surgery. It integrates basic science with clinical subspecialty knowledge.

framing.relevance: Subspecialty clinical medicine domain covering musculoskeletal surgical and non-surgical management. Sibling to Musculoskeletal and Rheumatology under clinical medicine.

content.title: Musculoskeletal Biomechanics Principles
content.definition: Application of mechanical principles to forces, moments, and material behavior in bone, tendon, ligament, and joint systems.
content.summary: Stress, strain, modulus, and moment arms govern implant loading and fracture fixation. Free-body analysis informs joint reaction forces and gait.

framing.relevance: Foundation for fracture fixation, arthroplasty stability, and implant design.

content.title: Implant Materials and Tribology
content.definition: Properties of metals, polymers, and ceramics used in orthopaedic implants, including wear mechanisms at bearing interfaces.
content.summary: Modulus mismatch drives stress shielding. Cross-linked polyethylene, ceramic-on-ceramic, and metal-on-metal bearings differ in wear and osteolysis risk.

framing.relevance: Core adult reconstruction and bearing selection knowledge.

content.title: Gait Analysis and Kinesiology
content.definition: Systematic study of human locomotion phases, joint kinematics, and kinetics during the gait cycle.
content.summary: Stance and swing phases divide gait. Hip, knee, and ankle moments peak at distinct phases. Pathologic gait patterns localize neuromuscular and structural deficits.

framing.relevance: ABOS Part I basic science; essential for CP, amputation, and arthroplasty assessment.

content.title: Orthopaedic Radiographic Imaging
content.definition: Standard radiographic views, positioning, and systematic interpretation of musculoskeletal plain films.
content.summary: AP and lateral views are baseline. Alignment, joint space, cortical integrity, and soft-tissue signs guide fracture and arthritis assessment.

framing.relevance: Gateway diagnostic skill across all orthopaedic subspecialties.

content.title: Advanced Musculoskeletal Imaging
content.definition: Cross-sectional and functional imaging modalities including CT, MRI, bone scan, and PET applied to musculoskeletal disorders.
content.summary: MRI excels for soft tissue and marrow edema. CT provides fracture detail and arthroplasty planning. Bone scan detects occult stress injury and metastases.

framing.relevance: Required for tumor workup, occult fracture, and soft-tissue pathology.

content.title: Orthopaedic Surgical Approaches and Anatomy
content.definition: Anatomic relationships and internervous planes defining safe surgical access to major musculoskeletal regions.
content.summary: Approaches respect neurovascular bundles and internervous planes. Draping, positioning, and incision planning minimize iatrogenic injury.

framing.relevance: ABOS Part I anatomy section; prerequisite for all operative subspecialties.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Orthopaedic Surgical Approaches and Anatomy

content.title: Soft Tissue Coverage and Flaps
content.definition: Principles of wound bed preparation and soft-tissue transfer for exposed bone, hardware, and complex extremity wounds.
content.summary: Local and free flaps restore vascularized coverage. Timing with fracture stabilization and infection control determines reconstructive success.

framing.relevance: Critical for open fractures, chronic osteomyelitis, and tumor resection defects.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Soft Tissue Coverage and Flaps

content.title: Compartment Syndrome and Fasciotomy
content.definition: Elevated intracompartmental pressure causing ischemic muscle and nerve injury, diagnosed clinically and treated with emergent fasciotomy.
content.summary: Pain out of proportion, pain with passive stretch, and tense compartments are hallmarks. Delayed diagnosis risks irreversible Volkmann ischemic contracture.

framing.relevance: Time-critical diagnosis in trauma and reperfusion injury.

content.title: Fracture Classification Systems
content.definition: Standardized schemes describing fracture location, pattern, displacement, and associated soft-tissue injury for communication and treatment planning.
content.summary: AO/OTA, Neer, Garden, and Gustilo classifications enable consistent documentation. Classification guides prognosis and operative indication.

framing.relevance: Foundation for all trauma subspecialty management.

content.title: Fracture Reduction and Fixation Principles
content.definition: Biomechanical and biologic principles guiding closed and open reduction, immobilization, and internal or external fixation of fractures.
content.summary: Absolute versus relative stability affects healing mode. Lag screws, plates, nails, and external fixators are selected by fracture personality and soft tissues.

framing.relevance: Core trauma knowledge integrating biomechanics and bone healing biology.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Fracture Reduction and Fixation Principles

source_references.section: SPRINT trial — reamed vs unreamed nailing

content.title: Open Fractures and Mangled Extremity
content.definition: Fractures communicating with the external environment through soft-tissue disruption, graded by wound severity and requiring urgent management.
content.summary: Gustilo classification guides antibiotic duration and soft-tissue coverage. MESS score aids mangled extremity salvage versus amputation decisions.

framing.relevance: High-yield ABOS trauma topic with time-sensitive management.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Open Fractures and Mangled Extremity

content.title: Polytrauma and Damage Control Orthopaedics
content.definition: Staged orthopaedic management in multiply injured patients prioritizing life-threatening injuries and physiologic stabilization before definitive fixation.
content.summary: Early total care versus damage control orthopaedics depends on ISS and physiologic status. External fixation temporizes long-bone fractures in unstable patients.

framing.relevance: ABOS multiple trauma section; integrates with ATLS principles.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Polytrauma and Damage Control Orthopaedics

content.title: Clavicle, Acromioclavicular, and Sternoclavicular Injuries
content.definition: Fractures and ligamentous disruptions of the clavicle, acromioclavicular joint, and sternoclavicular region from direct or indirect force.
content.summary: Midshaft clavicle, AC separations (Rockwood), and SC dislocations including posterior SC mediastinal risk. Brachial plexus injury may accompany clavicle trauma.

framing.relevance: ABOS upper extremity trauma; overlaps shoulder/elbow subspecialty.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Clavicle, Acromioclavicular, and Sternoclavicular Injuries

content.title: Proximal Humerus Fractures
content.definition: Fractures of the surgical neck and tuberosities of the proximal humerus, classified by fragment number and displacement.
content.summary: Neer classification guides treatment. Low-demand elderly patients may be managed nonoperatively; displaced multi-part fractures often require ORIF or arthroplasty.

framing.relevance: Common fragility fracture with complex decision-making.

content.title: Humerus Shaft and Elbow Fractures
content.definition: Diaphyseal humerus fractures and intra-articular distal humerus, radial head, and olecranon fractures around the elbow.
content.summary: Radial nerve palsy complicates humerus shaft fractures. Distal humerus fractures often require dual-plate fixation. Radial head fractures follow Mason classification.

framing.relevance: Elbow fracture-dislocations require urgent assessment of instability.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Humerus Shaft and Elbow Fractures

content.title: Forearm Fractures and Monteggia Injuries
content.definition: Radius and ulna shaft fractures and Monteggia fracture-dislocations involving ulnar fracture with radial head dislocation.
content.summary: Anatomic restoration of forearm rotation is critical. Monteggia injuries require radial head reduction and ulnar fixation to restore stability.

framing.relevance: Longitudinal forearm instability affects supination-pronation arc.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Forearm Fractures and Monteggia Injuries

content.title: Pelvic and Acetabular Fractures
content.definition: High-energy injuries to the pelvic ring and acetabulum disrupting stability and potentially causing hemorrhage and visceral injury.
content.summary: Young-Burgess and Tile classifications describe pelvic ring injuries. Acetabular fractures follow Letournel classification; operative timing depends on hemodynamic status.

framing.relevance: High-energy trauma with multidisciplinary management.

content.title: Hip Fractures and Dislocations
content.definition: Fractures of the femoral head, neck, intertrochanteric, and subtrochanteric regions and traumatic hip dislocations.
content.summary: Femoral neck fractures in young patients require anatomic fixation; in elderly often arthroplasty. Intertrochanteric fractures use cephalomedullary or sliding hip screw fixation.

framing.relevance: Common fragility fracture with significant morbidity and mortality.

content.title: Femoral Shaft Fractures
content.definition: Diaphyseal femur fractures typically from high-energy trauma, managed with intramedullary nailing or plating.
content.summary: Reamed intramedullary nailing is standard for closed fractures. Floating knee and ipsilateral neck/shaft fractures require specialized fixation strategies.

framing.relevance: Common long-bone fracture; SPRINT trial informs reaming practice.

content.title: Tibial Plateau Fractures
content.definition: Intra-articular fractures of the proximal tibia involving the weight-bearing articular surface and adjacent metaphysis.
content.summary: Schatzker classification guides approach and fixation. Meniscus and ligament injuries are common associated injuries requiring assessment.

framing.relevance: Articular fracture requiring anatomic reduction to prevent post-traumatic arthritis.

content.title: Tibia and Fibula Shaft Fractures
content.definition: Diaphyseal fractures of the tibia and fibula including open injuries with compartment syndrome risk.
content.summary: Tibia is subcutaneous with high open fracture rate. Intramedullary nailing is preferred for closed diaphyseal fractures when soft tissues allow.

framing.relevance: Compartment syndrome monitoring is mandatory.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Tibia and Fibula Shaft Fractures

content.title: Pilon and Ankle Fractures
content.definition: Distal tibial plafond (pilon) fractures and malleolar ankle fractures disrupting the mortise and syndesmosis.
content.summary: Ankle fractures follow Lauge-Hansen and Weber classifications. Syndesmosis integrity determines fixation. Pilon fractures often require staged ORIF.

framing.relevance: Ankle mortise restoration is essential for function.

content.title: Spine Trauma — Fractures and Instability
content.definition: Traumatic vertebral column injuries including fracture-dislocations, burst fractures, and ligamentous instability with spinal cord injury risk.
content.summary: TLICS and SLIC scores guide operative versus nonoperative management for thoracolumbar and subaxial cervical injuries. Occipitocervical dissociation is unstable. Spinal cord injury requires hemodynamic support; cervical and thoracolumbar detail at L4.

framing.relevance: Overlaps spine subspecialty; distinct trauma management algorithms.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Spine Trauma — Fractures and Instability

content.title: Stress Fractures and Insufficiency Fractures
content.definition: Fatigue fractures from repetitive loading and insufficiency fractures in abnormal or osteoporotic bone under normal stress.
content.summary: High-risk locations include femoral neck tension side, anterior tibia, and navicular. Insufficiency pelvic and sacral fractures occur in elderly osteoporotic patients.

knowledge_graph._shared_concept_note: Shared with foot/ankle high-risk locations (navicular, metatarsals) and osteoporosis/insufficiency fractures — cross-link in foot/ankle hub and osteoporosis existing ref.

framing.relevance: Sports medicine and fragility fracture overlap.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Stress Fractures and Insufficiency Fractures

content.title: Malunion, Nonunion, and Delayed Union
content.definition: Failure of fracture healing with deformity (malunion), absence of healing (nonunion), or prolonged healing (delayed union).
content.summary: Hypertrophic nonunion may respond to stabilization; atrophic nonunion requires biologic augmentation. Malunion correction uses osteotomy.

framing.relevance: Common complication management across trauma practice.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Malunion, Nonunion, and Delayed Union

content.title: Fat Embolism and Trauma Complications
content.definition: Systemic complications of major orthopaedic trauma including fat embolism syndrome, ARDS, and systemic inflammatory response.
content.summary: Fat embolism presents with hypoxia, neurologic changes, and petechiae after long-bone fractures. Early stabilization and supportive care are mainstays.

framing.relevance: ABOS multiple trauma section; links to critical care.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Fat Embolism and Trauma Complications

content.title: Brachial Plexus Injuries — Trauma and Reconstruction
content.definition: Traumatic or obstetric injury to the brachial plexus nerve roots and trunks causing upper extremity weakness, sensory loss, and dysfunction.
content.summary: Erb-Duchenne and Klumpke patterns localize injury. Repair, grafting, and nerve transfer restore function when reinnervation is limited. Sunderland classification guides prognosis.

framing.title_in_context: Brachial Plexus Injuries — Trauma and Reconstruction
framing.relevance: Links trauma, peds, and peripheral nerve subspecialties.

hierarchy_location.topic: Brachial Plexus Injuries — Trauma and Reconstruction

source_references.source: StatPearls — Brachial Plexus Injuries — Trauma and Reconstruction

content.title: Hip Joint Biomechanics and Arthropathy
content.definition: Anatomic and biomechanical features of the hip joint and degenerative, inflammatory, and avascular pathologies leading to end-stage disease.
content.summary: Hip reaction force depends on abductor moment arm. Osteoarthritis, AVN, and post-traumatic arthritis are common THA indications.

framing.relevance: Prerequisite for hip arthroplasty decision-making.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Hip Joint Biomechanics and Arthropathy

content.title: Knee Joint Biomechanics and Arthropathy
content.definition: Knee kinematics, load distribution, and arthropathies including osteoarthritis, inflammatory arthritis, and post-traumatic degeneration.
content.summary: Varus and valgus alignment alter compartment loading. Knee OA management progresses from conservative care to arthroplasty based on severity and alignment.

knowledge_graph._shared_concept_note: General OA principles exist in spine_medicine_clinical_l3_osteoarthritis — this node emphasizes surgical arthropathy.

framing.relevance: Foundation for TKA and unicompartmental arthroplasty.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Knee Joint Biomechanics and Arthropathy

content.title: Total Hip Arthroplasty
content.definition: Surgical replacement of the femoral head and acetabulum with prosthetic components to restore joint function and relieve pain in end-stage hip disease.
content.summary: Approach, bearing selection, and component positioning determine stability and wear. Complications include dislocation, infection, and periprosthetic fracture.

framing.relevance: Core ABOS adult reconstruction topic.

content.title: Total Knee Arthroplasty
content.definition: Resurfacing of femoral, tibial, and patellar articular surfaces with prosthetic components for end-stage knee arthritis.
content.summary: Mechanical alignment versus kinematic alignment philosophies guide component placement. ROM, stability, and patellar tracking determine outcomes.

framing.relevance: Among the most common elective orthopaedic procedures.

content.title: Unicompartmental and Partial Joint Replacement
content.definition: Replacement of a single articular compartment or hemiarthroplasty of the femoral head for localized joint disease.
content.summary: UKA requires intact ACL and single-compartment disease. Hip hemiarthroplasty is used for displaced femoral neck fractures in selected elderly patients.

framing.relevance: ABOS hip and knee reconstruction subsection.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Unicompartmental and Partial Joint Replacement

content.title: Revision Arthroplasty
content.definition: Reoperation to exchange, augment, or remove failed joint replacement components for infection, instability, loosening, or wear.
content.summary: Workup distinguishes septic from aseptic failure. Bone loss classification guides reconstruction with stems, cones, and augments.

framing.relevance: High-complexity ABOS topic appearing in clinical vignettes.

content.title: Bearing Surfaces and Implant Tribology
content.definition: Articulating material combinations in joint replacement and their wear characteristics, osteolysis potential, and clinical longevity.
content.summary: Metal-on-polyethylene, ceramic-on-ceramic, and highly cross-linked polyethylene reduce wear at different cost profiles. Trunnion corrosion affects metal-on-metal hips.

framing.relevance: Direct ABOS reconstruction and basic science crossover.
applications: Bearing selection by age and activity

source_references.source: StatPearls — Bearing Surfaces and Implant Tribology

content.title: Periprosthetic Joint Infection — Hip and Knee
content.definition: Infection involving joint replacement hardware requiring diagnostic workup and single- or two-stage revision with prolonged antibiotic therapy.
content.summary: MSIS criteria combine serology, synovial fluid, and intraoperative findings. DAIR for acute infections; chronic often requires two-stage exchange for hip and knee arthroplasty.

framing.title_in_context: Periprosthetic Joint Infection — Hip and Knee
framing.relevance: High-yield complication with defined diagnostic criteria.

hierarchy_location.topic: Periprosthetic Joint Infection — Hip and Knee

source_references.source: StatPearls — Periprosthetic Joint Infection — Hip and Knee

content.title: Periprosthetic Fracture Management
content.definition: Fractures adjacent to or involving joint replacement components classified by location, stability, and bone stock.
content.summary: Vancouver classification guides hip periprosthetic fracture management. Intraoperative versus postoperative fractures differ in treatment options.

framing.relevance: ABOS femur subsection; increasing incidence with aging population.

content.title: Hip and Knee Osteonecrosis
content.definition: Ischemic death of subchondral bone leading to collapse and secondary arthritis, with etiologies including idiopathic, steroid, alcohol, and trauma.
content.summary: Ficat and Steinberg staging guide hip ON treatment from core decompression to arthroplasty. Collapse predicts poor joint-preserving outcomes.

framing.relevance: Common THA indication in younger patients.

content.title: Cervical Spine Degenerative Disease
content.definition: Age-related cervical spondylosis causing radiculopathy, myelopathy, and axial neck pain from disc degeneration and osteophyte formation.
content.summary: Myelopathy presents with gait disturbance, hyperreflexia, and Hoffman sign. ACDF and posterior decompression/fusion are main surgical options.

framing.relevance: ABOS cervical spine 4% weight.

content.title: Craniocervical Junction Disorders
content.definition: Congenital and acquired disorders of the occiput-C2 region including basilar invagination, odontoid pathology, and rheumatoid instability.
content.summary: Rheumatoid pannus causes atlantoaxial instability. Odontoid fractures in elderly may be managed operatively or with C1-C2 fusion.

framing.relevance: ABOS occiput-C2 subsection.

content.title: Thoracic Spine Disorders
content.definition: Pathology of the thoracic and thoracolumbar spine including fracture, deformity, disc herniation, and inflammatory disease.
content.summary: Thoracic disc herniation presents with myelopathy. Scheuermann kyphosis and thoracic deformity require sagittal balance assessment.

framing.relevance: Lower ABOS weight but testable in deformity contexts.

content.title: Lumbar Disc Herniation and Radiculopathy
content.definition: Displacement of lumbar intervertebral disc material causing nerve root compression and radicular pain or neurologic deficit.
content.summary: Most disc herniations improve conservatively. Cauda equina syndrome requires emergent decompression. Surgical options include microdiscectomy.

framing.relevance: Common clinical presentation; overlaps general back pain red flags.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Lumbar Disc Herniation and Radiculopathy

content.title: Lumbar Spinal Stenosis
content.definition: Narrowing of the lumbar spinal canal or neural foramina causing neurogenic claudication and radiculopathy in older adults.
content.summary: Neurogenic claudication improves with flexion (shopping cart sign). Decompression with or without fusion depends on spondylolisthesis and instability.

framing.relevance: High-volume degenerative spine pathology.

content.title: Spondylolisthesis
content.definition: Anterior slippage of one vertebral body on another from isthmic, degenerative, or traumatic causes.
content.summary: Isthmic spondylolisthesis affects young athletes; degenerative affects older adults. High-grade slips may require fusion with reduction.

framing.relevance: ABOS lumbar and sports spine crossover.

content.title: Adult Spinal Deformity
content.definition: Sagittal and coronal plane malalignment in skeletally mature patients including degenerative scoliosis and fixed kyphosis.
content.summary: SVA, PT, and PI-LL mismatch predict disability. Surgery balances correction with complication risk including pseudarthrosis and junctional failure.

framing.relevance: ABOS deformity section; growing subspecialty.

content.title: Spinal Cord Injury Syndromes
content.definition: Partial and complete spinal cord injury patterns including central cord, anterior cord, Brown-Séquard, and cauda equina syndromes.
content.summary: Central cord syndrome affects upper extremities more than lower. ASIA impairment scale documents neurologic level and completeness.

framing.relevance: ABOS nonspecific spine section; critical for trauma and degenerative emergencies.

content.title: Spinal Infections and Inflammatory Disease
content.definition: Discitis, osteomyelitis, epidural abscess, and spondyloarthropathies affecting the vertebral column.
content.summary: MRI is diagnostic for spinal infection. Biopsy guides antibiotics. Ankylosing spondylitis causes fusion and fracture risk through brittle spine.

framing.relevance: Infection management overlaps general ID; orthopaedic surgical indications distinct.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Spinal Infections and Inflammatory Disease

content.title: Spinal Fusion and Instrumentation Principles
content.definition: Biomechanical and biologic principles of arthrodesis including graft selection, interbody fusion, and pedicle screw fixation.
content.summary: 360-degree fusion combines anterior support with posterior tension band. Pedicle screw density and rod contour affect construct stiffness.

framing.relevance: Operative foundation for spine surgery subspecialty.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Spinal Fusion and Instrumentation Principles

content.title: Anterior Cruciate Ligament Injury
content.definition: Tear of the anterior cruciate ligament destabilizing the knee in rotation and anterior translation, common in pivoting sports.
content.summary: Pivot shift and Lachman tests suggest ACL injury. MRI confirms tear pattern. Reconstruction uses autograft or allograft with tunnel placement critical to kinematics.

framing.relevance: Highest-yield sports medicine knee topic on ABOS.

content.title: Posterior Cruciate and Collateral Ligament Injuries
content.definition: Injuries to the PCL, MCL, LCL, and posterolateral corner causing varus, valgus, or posterior instability of the knee.
content.summary: PCL injuries often from dashboard injury. PLC injuries require anatomic reconstruction. Combined ligament injuries have higher stiffness risk.

framing.title_in_context: Posterior Cruciate and Collateral Ligament Injuries
framing.relevance: ABOS knee dislocations/instability 2% weight within sports.

hierarchy_location.topic: Posterior Cruciate and Collateral Ligament Injuries

source_references.source: StatPearls — Posterior Cruciate and Collateral Ligament Injuries

content.title: Meniscal Tears and Management
content.definition: Tears of the fibrocartilaginous meniscus from acute trauma or degenerative change affecting knee mechanics and pain.
content.summary: Red-white zone vascularity determines healing potential. Repair is preferred in young patients with vertical tears; partial meniscectomy for irreparable patterns.

framing.relevance: Cartilage/meniscus ABOS sports section.

content.title: Shoulder Instability and Labral Injuries
content.definition: Traumatic or atraumatic glenohumeral subluxation and dislocation with associated labral and capsular pathology.
content.summary: Bankart lesions occur with anterior instability. Hill-Sachs and engaging lesions affect recurrence risk. Latarjet addresses bone loss.

framing.relevance: Core sports shoulder topic overlapping shoulder/elbow subspecialty.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Shoulder Instability and Labral Injuries

content.title: Rotator Cuff Pathology
content.definition: Tendinopathy, partial-thickness, and full-thickness tears of the rotator cuff tendons causing pain and weakness.
content.summary: Subacromial impingement and cuff tears present with night pain and weakness. MRI quantifies tear size and retraction guiding repair versus debridement.

framing.relevance: Heavily tested; MOON cohort informs repair decisions.

source_references.section: MOON Shoulder Group — rotator cuff outcomes

content.title: Patellofemoral Instability and Disorders
content.definition: Lateral patellar dislocation, maltracking, and anterior knee pain from patellofemoral joint dysplasia and soft-tissue imbalance.
content.summary: TT-TG distance and trochlear dysplasia guide instability surgery. MPFL reconstruction restores restraint in recurrent dislocators.

framing.relevance: ABOS extensor mechanism and patellofemoral section.

content.title: Extensor Mechanism Knee Injuries
content.definition: Disruption of the quadriceps tendon, patellar tendon, or patella fracture compromising knee extension.
content.summary: Patellar tendon and quadriceps tears require operative repair. Bipartite patella mimics fracture. Stiffness and rerupture are complications.

framing.relevance: ABOS sports knee extensor mechanism 2% weight.

content.title: Cartilage Injuries and Restoration
content.definition: Focal articular cartilage defects and osteochondral injuries managed with marrow stimulation, grafting, or cell-based therapies.
content.summary: Microfracture, OATS, and MACI are options by defect size and location. OCD lesions in adolescents may require drilling or fixation.

framing.relevance: ABOS cartilage/meniscus section.
applications: Return to sport after cartilage procedure

content.title: Sports Medicine Medical Conditions
content.definition: Non-musculoskeletal conditions affecting athletes including concussion, cardiac screening, heat illness, and endocrine disorders.
content.summary: Return-to-play protocols follow concussion guidelines. Female athlete triad and RED-S affect bone health. Sickle cell trait affects exertional collapse.

framing.relevance: ABOS medical aspects of sports medicine 1%.

content.title: Glenohumeral Arthritis — Non-Arthroplasty Management
content.definition: Degenerative, inflammatory, and post-traumatic glenohumeral arthritis managed conservatively or with joint-preserving surgery.
content.summary: Young patients may undergo arthroscopic debridement or interposition. Cuff tear arthropathy requires reverse total shoulder arthroplasty rather than anatomic replacement.

framing.relevance: Shoulder joint arthritis ABOS section before arthroplasty nodes.

content.title: Primary Shoulder Arthroplasty
content.definition: Anatomic total shoulder, hemiarthroplasty, and reverse total shoulder replacement for end-stage glenohumeral disease.
content.summary: Reverse TSA is indicated for cuff tear arthropathy and complex fractures. Component version and glenosphere position affect stability.

framing.relevance: ABOS primary shoulder arthroplasty 1%.

content.title: Revision Shoulder Arthroplasty
content.definition: Reoperation for failed shoulder replacement due to infection, instability, loosening, or component malposition.
content.summary: Instability after anatomic TSA may require conversion to reverse. Periprosthetic infection follows similar principles to hip and knee.

framing.relevance: ABOS revision shoulder arthroplasty section.

content.title: Shoulder Stiffness and Capsular Disorders
content.definition: Limited glenohumeral motion from adhesive capsulitis, postoperative or post-traumatic stiffness, and calcific tendinopathy.
content.summary: Adhesive capsulitis progresses through freezing and thawing phases. Manipulation under anesthesia and capsular release are options for refractory stiffness.

framing.relevance: ABOS shoulder other conditions section.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Shoulder Stiffness and Capsular Disorders

content.title: Elbow Arthritis and Arthroplasty
content.definition: Degenerative and post-traumatic elbow arthritis managed with debridement, interposition, or total elbow arthroplasty.
content.summary: TEA is reserved for low-demand patients with rheumatoid or post-traumatic arthritis. Linked versus unlinked designs differ in stability.

framing.relevance: ABOS elbow joint arthritis section.

content.title: Elbow Instability — Chronic and Recurrent
content.definition: Chronic medial collateral ligament insufficiency and posterolateral rotatory instability of the elbow.
content.summary: UCL reconstruction restores valgus stability in throwers. PLRI results from LCL complex disruption; anatomic reconstruction restores rotatory stability.

framing.relevance: Overlaps sports medicine elbow section.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Elbow Instability — Chronic and Recurrent

content.title: Proximal Biceps and Throwing Shoulder
content.definition: Pathology of the long head of biceps and overhead athlete shoulder including internal impingement and GIRD.
content.summary: SLAP tears and biceps tenodesis versus tenotomy are debated in overhead athletes. GIRD and internal impingement contribute to labral pathology.

framing.relevance: ABOS throwing shoulder subsection.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Proximal Biceps and Throwing Shoulder

content.title: Distal Radius Fractures
content.definition: Fractures of the distal radius and ulna from fall on outstretched hand, classified by displacement and articular involvement.
content.summary: AO and Frye classifications guide treatment. ORIF with volar locking plate is common for displaced fractures. Malunion causes DRUJ dysfunction.

framing.relevance: Highest-volume hand/wrist fracture; ABOS wrist fracture 1.5%.

content.title: Carpal Fractures and Instability
content.definition: Scaphoid and other carpal fractures and perilunate injury patterns disrupting carpal alignment.
content.summary: Scaphoid waist fractures risk nonunion and AVN. Perilunate and lunate dislocations require urgent reduction and fixation.

framing.relevance: ABOS carpus fracture section.

content.title: Wrist Arthritis and Arthropathy
content.definition: Degenerative, post-traumatic, inflammatory, and septic arthritis of the radiocarpal and distal radioulnar joints.
content.summary: SLAC and SNAC wrists progress from scaphoid or ligament pathology. Partial and total wrist arthrodesis or arthroplasty are salvage options.

framing.relevance: ABOS wrist arthritis 1%.

content.title: Flexor Tendon Injuries and Repair
content.definition: Laceration or rupture of flexor digitorum and flexor pollicis longus tendons within the fibro-osseous sheath.
content.summary: Zone II (no man's land) repairs require core and epitendinous sutures. Early protected motion protocols reduce adhesions.

framing.relevance: ABOS hand tendon section.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Flexor Tendon Injuries and Repair

content.title: Extensor Tendon Injuries and Repair
content.definition: Disruption of extensor tendons over the dorsum of the hand and wrist from laceration or attrition.
content.summary: Extensor zones guide repair approach. Mallet finger may be managed with splinting; sagittal band rupture causes extensor subluxation.

framing.relevance: Complements flexor tendon cluster for hand surgery.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Extensor Tendon Injuries and Repair

content.title: Nerve Compression Syndromes of the Upper Extremity
content.definition: Entrapment neuropathies including carpal tunnel, cubital tunnel, and radial tunnel syndromes causing pain and dysfunction.
content.summary: EMG/NCS confirms diagnosis and severity. Carpal tunnel release is most common; cubital tunnel options include in situ release and transposition.

framing.title_in_context: Nerve Compression Syndromes of the Upper Extremity
framing.relevance: ABOS wrist and hand nerve section.

hierarchy_location.topic: Nerve Compression Syndromes of the Upper Extremity

source_references.source: StatPearls — Nerve Compression Syndromes of the Upper Extremity

content.title: Hand Fractures and Dislocations
content.definition: Fractures of metacarpals and phalanges and dislocations of MCP, PIP, and DIP joints including thumb CMC instability.
content.summary: Boxer's fracture of fifth metacarpal neck often managed closed. Bennett and Rolando fractures require thumb CMC stabilization. Gamekeeper's thumb needs UCL repair.

framing.relevance: ABOS hand fracture 1%.

content.title: Dupuytren Disease and Hand Contractures
content.definition: Fibroproliferative disorder of palmar fascia and other contractures including Volkmann ischemic contracture and intrinsic tightness.
content.summary: Dupuytren cords cause MCP and PIP contracture; collagenase and fasciectomy are treatment options. Volkmann contracture follows compartment syndrome.

framing.relevance: ABOS contracture section.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Dupuytren Disease and Hand Contractures

content.title: Wrist Ligament and TFCC Injuries
content.definition: Tears of intrinsic wrist ligaments and triangular fibrocartilage complex causing ulnar-sided wrist pain and instability.
content.summary: Scapholunate dissociation leads to DISI deformity. TFCC tears cause DRUJ instability; foveal tears have worse prognosis.

framing.relevance: ABOS TFCC and wrist instability section.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Wrist Ligament and TFCC Injuries

content.title: Wrist and Hand Infections
content.definition: Septic arthritis, flexor tenosynovitis, felon, and deep space infections of the hand requiring urgent drainage.
content.summary: Kanavel signs diagnose flexor tenosynovitis. Human bites and IV drug use are high-risk. Emergent irrigation prevents tendon and joint destruction.

framing.relevance: Time-critical hand surgery topic.

content.title: Ankle Fractures and Pilon Injuries
content.definition: Malleolar ankle fractures and intra-articular distal tibial pilon fractures disrupting the ankle mortise.
content.summary: Anatomic reduction of the mortise and syndesmosis restoration are goals. Pilon fractures often use staged ORIF after soft-tissue recovery.

framing.relevance: ABOS ankle/leg fracture 2%.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Ankle Fractures and Pilon Injuries

content.title: Ankle Sprains and Chronic Instability
content.definition: Lateral and medial ligament sprains of the ankle and chronic functional or mechanical instability after incomplete healing.
content.summary: ATFL and CFL are commonly injured in inversion. Chronic instability may require Broström repair or ligament reconstruction.

framing.relevance: ABOS ankle sprain section 1%.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Ankle Sprains and Chronic Instability

content.title: Achilles Tendon Disorders
content.definition: Achilles tendinopathy and acute or chronic rupture of the Achilles tendon affecting plantarflexion power.
content.summary: Acute rupture managed operatively or nonoperatively by patient factors. Chronic rupture may require tendon transfer or lengthening.

framing.relevance: ABOS tendon/muscle ankle section.

content.title: Total Ankle Arthroplasty and Ankle Arthrodesis
content.definition: Surgical treatment of end-stage ankle arthritis with joint replacement or fusion.
content.summary: TAA preserves motion in appropriate candidates; arthrodesis remains gold standard for deformity and heavy demand. Alignment guides fusion technique.

framing.title_in_context: Total Ankle Arthroplasty and Ankle Arthrodesis
framing.relevance: ABOS ankle arthritis section.

hierarchy_location.topic: Total Ankle Arthroplasty and Ankle Arthrodesis

source_references.source: StatPearls — Total Ankle Arthroplasty and Ankle Arthrodesis

content.title: Hindfoot Fractures and Disorders
content.definition: Calcaneus, talus, and navicular fractures and hindfoot alignment disorders affecting gait.
content.summary: Calcaneus fractures correlate with axial loading and may be intra-articular. Talus fractures risk AVN. Hawkins sign indicates vascularity.

framing.relevance: ABOS hindfoot fracture section.

content.title: Plantar Fasciitis and Heel Pain
content.definition: Plantar fascial overload causing medial heel pain and related calcaneal stress and bony pathology.
content.summary: First-step pain is characteristic. Conservative care includes stretching, orthotics, and night splints. Calcaneal stress fracture must be excluded.

framing.relevance: Common outpatient foot complaint.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Plantar Fasciitis and Heel Pain

content.title: Adult Acquired Flatfoot Deformity
content.definition: Progressive loss of medial longitudinal arch from posterior tibial tendon insufficiency and peritalar subluxation.
content.summary: Johnson and Strom stages guide treatment from orthotics to tendon transfer and fusion. Flexible versus rigid deformity affects surgical plan.

framing.relevance: ABOS posterior tibial dysfunction section.

content.title: Forefoot Deformities and Disorders
content.definition: Hallux valgus, hallux rigidus, lesser toe deformities, metatarsalgia, and neuroma causing forefoot pain and dysfunction.
content.summary: Hallux valgus angle and IM angle guide bunion surgery. Cheilectomy versus fusion for hallux rigidus depends on arthritis severity.

framing.relevance: ABOS forefoot 1% weight.

content.title: Lisfranc and Midfoot Injuries
content.definition: Tarsometatarsal joint complex injuries from direct or indirect force causing instability of the midfoot arch.
content.summary: Missed Lisfranc injuries cause chronic pain and collapse. ORIF versus primary fusion depends on instability pattern.

framing.relevance: ABOS midfoot section; sports and trauma crossover.

content.title: Developmental Dysplasia of the Hip
content.definition: Abnormal development of the hip joint ranging from dysplasia to subluxation and dislocation in infancy.
content.summary: Ortolani and Barlow maneuvers screen neonates. Pavlik harness treats reducible dislocations; open reduction for failed closed treatment.

framing.relevance: Most common paediatric hip topic on ABOS.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Developmental Dysplasia of the Hip

content.title: Legg-Calvé-Perthes Disease
content.definition: Idiopathic avascular necrosis of the femoral head in children causing hip pain and potential femoral head deformity.
content.summary: Age at onset and Herring lateral pillar classification guide containment treatment. Goal is spherical femoral head at skeletal maturity.

framing.relevance: ABOS hip developmental section.

content.title: Slipped Capital Femoral Epiphysis
content.definition: Slippage of the femoral epiphysis relative to the metaphysis through the physis in overweight adolescents.
content.summary: Stable versus unstable slips differ in AVN risk. In situ pinning is standard; urgent treatment of unstable slips.

framing.relevance: High-yield paediatric hip emergency.

content.title: Clubfoot and Congenital Foot Deformities
content.definition: Congenital talipes equinovarus and other congenital foot anomalies including vertical talus and tarsal coalition.
content.summary: Ponseti serial casting and tenotomy correct most clubfeet. Vertical talus requires early reduction. Coalitions present with adolescent flatfoot pain.

framing.relevance: ABOS paediatric foot section.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Clubfoot and Congenital Foot Deformities

content.title: Paediatric Fractures and Physeal Injuries
content.definition: Fractures in growing bone classified by Salter-Harris involving the physis with unique remodeling and growth disturbance risk.
content.summary: Salter-Harris I-II often managed closed; III-IV require anatomic reduction. Physeal bar resection may prevent angular deformity.

framing.relevance: Foundation for all paediatric trauma.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Paediatric Fractures and Physeal Injuries

content.title: Supracondylar and Elbow Fractures — Paediatric
content.definition: Supracondylar humerus, lateral condyle, medial epicondyle, and radial neck fractures in children with vascular and nerve risk.
content.summary: Gartland classification guides supracondylar management. Brachial artery and AIN injury require monitoring. CRPP is common for displaced fractures.

framing.relevance: Most common paediatric fracture around elbow.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Supracondylar and Elbow Fractures — Paediatric

content.title: Paediatric Spine Deformity — Scoliosis
content.definition: Idiopathic and congenital spinal curvature in children and adolescents requiring bracing or surgical correction.
content.summary: Lenke classification guides adolescent idiopathic scoliosis surgery. Early-onset scoliosis may use growing rods. Congenital scoliosis from vertebral anomalies.

framing.relevance: ABOS paediatric spine 1.5%.

content.title: Paediatric Spondylolysis and Spondylolisthesis
content.definition: Pars interarticularis defects and vertebral slippage in children and adolescents, often in athletic populations.
content.summary: Activity modification and bracing treat low-grade isthmic slips in adolescents. High-grade slips may require fusion in situ or reduction.

framing.relevance: Sports and paediatric spine crossover.

content.title: Spina Bifida and Myelomeningocele — Orthopaedic Management
content.definition: Congenital neural tube defects causing paralytic deformities of the lower extremities requiring bracing and corrective surgery.
content.summary: Hip dislocation and equinovarus foot are common. Ambulatory potential depends on neurologic level. Late spinal deformity requires fusion.

framing.title_in_context: Spina Bifida and Myelomeningocele — Orthopaedic Management
framing.relevance: ABOS paediatric neuromuscular section.

hierarchy_location.topic: Spina Bifida and Myelomeningocele — Orthopaedic Management

source_references.source: StatPearls — Spina Bifida and Myelomeningocele — Orthopaedic Management

content.title: Limb Length Discrepancy and Angular Deformity
content.definition: Leg length inequality and coronal or sagittal angular deformities from congenital, developmental, or post-traumatic causes.
content.summary: Moseley charting predicts growth remaining. Guided growth for angular correction; epiphysiodesis or lengthening for LLD.

framing.title_in_context: Limb Length Discrepancy and Angular Deformity
framing.relevance: ABOS torsional and angular deformity section.

hierarchy_location.topic: Limb Length Discrepancy and Angular Deformity

source_references.source: StatPearls — Limb Length Discrepancy and Angular Deformity

content.title: Paediatric Sports and Overuse Injuries
content.definition: Apophysitis, stress fractures, and osteochondritis dissecans in skeletally immature athletes including Little Leaguer elbow.
content.summary: Osgood-Schlatter and Sever disease are traction apophysitis. Little Leaguer elbow involves medial epicondyle apophysitis. OCD may need drilling or fixation.

framing.relevance: ABOS paediatric sports 1.5%.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Paediatric Sports and Overuse Injuries

content.title: Osteogenesis Imperfecta and Skeletal Dysplasias
content.definition: Genetic disorders of connective tissue and bone matrix causing fragility, deformity, and short stature.
content.summary: OI types differ in severity and hearing loss risk. Intramedullary rodding prevents fracture cycles. Bisphosphonates reduce fracture rate in OI.

framing.relevance: ABOS paediatric general syndromes section.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Osteogenesis Imperfecta and Skeletal Dysplasias

content.title: Bone Tumor Evaluation and Biopsy
content.definition: Systematic radiographic and histologic evaluation of bone lesions including biopsy planning to avoid contamination.
content.summary: Age and location narrow differential. Biopsy tract must be excisable. Enneking staging applies to musculoskeletal tumors.

framing.relevance: Foundation for all oncology nodes; ABOS imaging/biopsy sections.

source_references.source: StatPearls — Bone Tumor Evaluation and Biopsy

content.title: Benign Bone Tumors
content.definition: Non-malignant bone lesions including osteochondroma, enchondroma, bone cysts, and giant cell tumor of bone.
content.summary: Osteochondroma is most common benign tumor. GCT is locally aggressive and may require curettage with adjuvants. Simple bone cysts occur in pediatric long bones.

framing.relevance: ABOS benign bone 2%.

content.title: Primary Malignant Bone Tumors
content.definition: Osteosarcoma, Ewing sarcoma, chondrosarcoma, and chordoma requiring multimodal therapy and limb salvage when possible.
content.summary: Osteosarcoma occurs in metaphysis of long bones in adolescents. Neoadjuvant chemotherapy and wide resection are standard. Ewing is radiosensitive.

framing.relevance: ABOS malignant bone 1%; high yield per topic.

content.title: Soft Tissue Sarcoma
content.definition: Malignant mesenchymal tumors of soft tissues requiring wide excision often combined with radiation.
content.summary: MRI defines compartment and neurovascular involvement. Margin status drives local recurrence. STS subtypes include liposarcoma and synovial sarcoma.

framing.relevance: ABOS malignant soft tissue 1%.

content.title: Benign Soft Tissue Tumors
content.definition: Lipoma, pigmented villonodular synovitis, nerve sheath tumors, and other benign soft-tissue masses.
content.summary: PVNS may be diffuse or localized; synovectomy is treatment. Lipomas are common; atypical features warrant MRI. Schwannomas arise from nerve sheath.

framing.relevance: ABOS benign soft tissue 0.5%.

content.title: Metastatic Bone Disease
content.definition: Secondary malignant involvement of bone from carcinoma, myeloma, or lymphoma causing pain, fracture, and hypercalcemia.
content.summary: Mirels score guides prophylactic fixation. Radiation and bisphosphonates/denosumab reduce skeletal events. Pathologic fracture through tumor needs stabilization.

framing.relevance: ABOS metastatic disease 2%.

content.title: Limb Salvage and Reconstruction in Oncology
content.definition: Techniques preserving limb function after tumor resection including endoprosthesis, allograft, and rotationplasty.
content.summary: Wide margin is oncologic priority. Endoprosthesis with expandable options used in skeletally immature patients. Allograft-prosthetic composite for segmental defects.

framing.title_in_context: Limb Salvage and Reconstruction in Oncology
framing.relevance: Integrates trauma, reconstruction, and oncology principles.

hierarchy_location.topic: Limb Salvage and Reconstruction in Oncology

source_references.source: StatPearls — Limb Salvage and Reconstruction in Oncology

content.title: Oncologic Syndromes and Staging
content.definition: Hereditary and syndromic associations with musculoskeletal tumors including MHE, Ollier disease, and Li-Fraumeni syndrome.
content.summary: Multiple hereditary exostoses carry chondrosarcoma risk. Maffucci syndrome links enchondromas with hemangiomas. Recognition guides surveillance.

framing.relevance: ABOS syndromes 0.5%.

content.title: Peripheral Neuropathy and Lower Extremity Nerve Entrapment
content.definition: Common peroneal, tarsal tunnel, and other lower extremity entrapment neuropathies and systemic neuropathy affecting gait.
content.summary: Foot drop from peroneal nerve palsy affects swing phase clearance. Tarsal tunnel syndrome causes medial ankle and plantar symptoms.

framing.title_in_context: Peripheral Neuropathy and Lower Extremity Nerve Entrapment
framing.relevance: ABOS foot and ankle nerve injury section.

hierarchy_location.topic: Peripheral Neuropathy and Lower Extremity Nerve Entrapment

source_references.source: StatPearls — Peripheral Neuropathy and Lower Extremity Nerve Entrapment

content.title: Muscular Dystrophy and Neuropathy — Orthopaedic Management
content.definition: Progressive neuromuscular disorders including Duchenne muscular dystrophy and Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease with orthopaedic deformities.
content.summary: DMD patients develop contractures and scoliosis; steroid therapy slows decline. CMT causes cavovarus foot requiring bracing or osteotomy.

framing.title_in_context: Muscular Dystrophy and Neuropathy — Orthopaedic Management
framing.relevance: ABOS paediatric neuromuscular 1%.

hierarchy_location.topic: Muscular Dystrophy and Neuropathy — Orthopaedic Management

source_references.source: StatPearls — Muscular Dystrophy and Neuropathy — Orthopaedic Management

content.title: Orthopaedic Rehabilitation Principles
content.definition: Structured recovery protocols after musculoskeletal injury and surgery emphasizing range of motion, strengthening, and functional progression.
content.summary: Phased rehabilitation protects healing tissue while preventing stiffness. Weight-bearing protocols differ by fixation stability and procedure type.

framing.relevance: Cross-cutting ABOS perioperative and subspecialty topic.

content.title: Fracture Healing and Bone Remodeling — Clinical Application
content.definition: Stages of bone repair from hematoma through remodeling and clinical factors affecting union in orthopaedic practice.
content.summary: Primary versus secondary bone healing depends on fixation stability. Nicotine, NSAIDs, and diabetes impair union. Remodeling continues long after clinical union.

framing.title_in_context: Fracture Healing and Bone Remodeling — Clinical Application
framing.relevance: Clinical counterpart to preclinical wound healing; core Part I basic science.

hierarchy_location.topic: Fracture Healing and Bone Remodeling — Clinical Application

source_references.source: StatPearls — Fracture Healing and Bone Remodeling — Clinical Application

content.title: General Paediatric Trauma
content.definition: Age-specific musculoskeletal trauma assessment including high-energy injury, non-accidental trauma, and child abuse recognition distinct from physeal fracture classification.
content.summary: Paediatric trauma requires ATLS resuscitation first. Non-accidental trauma patterns include metaphyseal corner and posterior rib fractures. Mandatory reporting when abuse is suspected.

framing.relevance: ABOS paediatric trauma and child protection content; home for NAT recognition per resident review.

content.title: Primary Survey — Orthopaedic Trauma
content.definition: Systematic ATLS-based assessment of life-threatening injuries and resuscitation priorities before definitive orthopaedic fracture management.
content.summary: ABCs take precedence over fracture fixation. Secondary survey identifies all injuries. Hemodynamic stability determines timing of definitive orthopaedic surgery versus damage control.

framing.relevance: Dedicated orthopaedic trauma topic — not anchored to general surgery primary survey node alone.

content.title: Muscle Contraction — Orthopaedic Application
content.definition: Sliding filament mechanics and force generation as applied to rehabilitation, tendon transfer, spasticity, and neuromuscular disorders in orthopaedic patients.
content.summary: Length-tension relationships guide rehab progression. Spasticity alters muscle architecture in CP and stroke. Tendon transfer redirects functional muscle units.

framing.relevance: Orthopaedic-specific muscle physiology beyond general preclinical overview.

content.title: Shoulder Periprosthetic Joint Infection
content.definition: Infection of shoulder replacement components with diagnostic workup and treatment distinct from hip and knee PJI.
content.summary: Propionibacterium acnes is shoulder-specific. Single-stage versus two-stage exchange depends on organism and chronicity. Resection arthroplasty is salvage option.

framing.relevance: Shoulder PJI biology and management differ from lower-extremity PJI.

content.title: Cerebral Palsy — Orthopaedic Management
content.definition: Musculoskeletal manifestations of cerebral palsy including spasticity, contractures, and gait abnormalities requiring multidisciplinary care.
content.summary: GMFCS level predicts ambulation. SEMLS addresses gait deviations. Hip surveillance prevents dislocation in non-ambulators.

knowledge_graph._shared_concept_note: Adult CP transition at L5 under cp_spasticity_and_contracture_management.

framing.relevance: Primary paediatric orthopaedic neuromuscular topic.

content.title: Charcot Neuroarthropathy
content.definition: Progressive osseous destruction and deformity of the foot and ankle from neuropathy, most commonly in diabetes.
content.summary: Acute Charcot presents as warm, swollen, red foot with minimal pain. Immobilization prevents rocker-bottom deformity. Eichenholtz stages guide treatment.

framing.relevance: Standalone foot and ankle topic per ABOS diabetic foot section; distinct from general diabetes management.

content.title: Diabetic Foot Ulcers and Limb Salvage
content.definition: Neuropathic and ischemic foot ulceration in diabetes requiring offloading, infection control, and multidisciplinary limb salvage.
content.summary: Wagner and UT classification guide depth and ischemia. Offloading is cornerstone. Osteomyelitis requires biopsy-directed antibiotics and possible resection.

framing.title_in_context: Diabetic Foot Ulcers and Limb Salvage
framing.relevance: Orthopaedic foot and ankle management of diabetic ulcers without conflating Charcot pathophysiology.

hierarchy_location.topic: Diabetic Foot Ulcers and Limb Salvage

source_references.source: StatPearls — Diabetic Foot Ulcers and Limb Salvage

content.title: Orthopaedic Basic Science
content.definition: Applied basic sciences supporting musculoskeletal diagnosis and treatment including biomechanics, imaging, implant science, and perioperative principles.
content.summary: Hub cluster for biomechanics, gait, musculoskeletal imaging, surgical approaches, soft-tissue biology, and fracture healing applied to orthopaedic practice.

framing.relevance: ABOS General Principles and basic science sections.

content.title: Orthopaedic Trauma
content.definition: Acute musculoskeletal injuries including fractures, dislocations, polytrauma, and complications of traumatic injury.
content.summary: Hub for fracture principles, regional trauma by extremity, spine trauma, and damage-control orthopaedics.

framing.relevance: ABOS Adult Trauma 13.5%.

content.title: Adult Reconstruction
content.definition: Elective joint replacement and revision surgery for end-stage arthropathy of the hip and knee.
content.summary: Hub for hip and knee arthropathy, arthroplasty, bearing surfaces, PJI, and periprosthetic fracture.

framing.relevance: ABOS Adult Reconstruction 8%.

content.title: Orthopaedic Spine
content.definition: Degenerative, deformity, infectious, and traumatic disorders of the vertebral column and spinal cord.
content.summary: Hub for cervical, thoracic, and lumbar pathology, spinal deformity, trauma, and fusion principles.

framing.relevance: ABOS Adult Spine 9%.

content.title: Orthopaedic Sports Medicine
content.definition: Athletic injuries of the musculoskeletal system emphasizing knee, medical aspects of sport, and cartilage restoration.
content.summary: Hub for knee ligament and meniscal injury, patellofemoral disorders, cartilage, and medical sports medicine.

framing.relevance: ABOS Sports Medicine 9.5%.

content.title: Shoulder and Elbow
content.definition: Non-traumatic and post-traumatic disorders of the shoulder and elbow including instability, cuff pathology, arthritis, and arthroplasty.
content.summary: Hub for shoulder instability, rotator cuff, arthroplasty, elbow arthritis, and throwing-athlete pathology.

framing.relevance: ABOS Shoulder and Elbow 7.5%.

content.title: Hand and Wrist
content.definition: Disorders of the hand and wrist including fracture, arthritis, tendon, nerve, and infection.
content.summary: Hub for distal radius and carpal injuries, tendon repairs, nerve compression, and hand infections.

framing.relevance: ABOS Hand 10.5%.

content.title: Foot and Ankle
content.definition: Disorders of the foot and ankle including fracture, instability, deformity, arthritis, and diabetic foot disease.
content.summary: Hub for ankle fracture, Achilles pathology, hindfoot and forefoot deformity, and Charcot arthropathy.

framing.relevance: ABOS Foot and Ankle 10%.

content.title: Paediatric Orthopaedics
content.definition: Musculoskeletal disorders in children including developmental hip conditions, physeal trauma, spine deformity, and neuromuscular disease.
content.summary: Hub for DDH, SCFE, Perthes, clubfoot, physeal fractures, paediatric spine, CP, and skeletal dysplasias.

framing.relevance: ABOS Pediatrics 12%.

content.title: Orthopaedic Oncology
content.definition: Benign and malignant tumors of bone and soft tissue including staging, biopsy, and limb salvage.
content.summary: Hub for tumor evaluation, primary bone malignancy, metastatic disease, sarcoma, and limb salvage.

framing.relevance: ABOS Oncology 7%.

content.title: Neuromuscular and Rehabilitation
content.definition: Neuromuscular disorders affecting the musculoskeletal system and rehabilitation principles after orthopaedic treatment.
content.summary: Hub for peripheral nerve entrapment, muscular dystrophy, brachial plexus, and rehabilitation protocols.

framing.relevance: ABOS neuromuscular and perioperative rehabilitation content.

abos_lens.name: ABOS Orthopaedic Surgery Board Certification Blueprint 2025
abos_lens.description: Curriculum lens over the universal spine. High-yield flags exist only in this lens (G60). Concept order follows subspecialty hub hierarchy then topic sequence (G59). Orthobullets lens should mirror hub structure separately (G58).
abos_lens.intended_audience: Orthopaedic surgery residents preparing for ABOS board certification

lens_architecture.orthobullets_lens_note: Extract parallel Orthobullets curriculum lens tied to same spine concept IDs with Orthobullets section hierarchy.
lens_architecture.exam_part: Part I subspecialty sufficient for combined prep

orthopaedic_context.relevance: Non-surgical musculoskeletal and rheumatology medicine sibling to orthopaedic surgery; shared conditions with distinct surgical subspecialty pathway.
applications: OA medical management before arthroplasty referral
orthopaedic_context.action_required: Add orthopaedic surgery sibling framing to MSK/Rheum L2 domain_context — clarifies boundary with spine_medicine_clinical_l2_orthopaedic_surgery.

orthopaedic_context.relevance: Wound healing biology underpins fracture healing, soft-tissue coverage, and surgical incision management in orthopaedics.
orthopaedic_context.action_required: Add medicine_clinical orthopaedic domain context to this existing node in the spine consolidation pass.

orthopaedic_context.relevance: Inflammatory cascades drive postoperative swelling, periprosthetic osteolysis, aseptic loosening, and synovitis in arthropathies. Osteolysis is a major arthroplasty failure mode.
orthopaedic_context.action_required: Add medicine_clinical orthopaedic domain context with emphasis on osteolysis and arthroplasty — high depth in ABOS/Orthobullets lens.

orthopaedic_context.relevance: Foundational MSK anatomy and physiology prerequisite for all orthopaedic subspecialty nodes.
orthopaedic_context.action_required: Add medicine_clinical orthopaedic domain context to this existing node in the spine consolidation pass.

orthopaedic_context.relevance: Collagen and matrix biology relate to ligament healing, tendon repair, and skeletal dysplasias.
orthopaedic_context.action_required: Add medicine_clinical orthopaedic domain context to this existing node in the spine consolidation pass.

orthopaedic_context.relevance: Sensitivity, specificity, and predictive values apply to orthopaedic screening tools and diagnostic tests.
orthopaedic_context.action_required: Add medicine_clinical orthopaedic domain context to this existing node in the spine consolidation pass.

orthopaedic_context.relevance: RCTs and observational studies underpin orthopaedic evidence including fixation trials and arthroplasty registries.
orthopaedic_context.action_required: Add medicine_clinical orthopaedic domain context to this existing node in the spine consolidation pass.

orthopaedic_context.relevance: Informed consent and disclosure apply to high-risk orthopaedic procedures including amputation and tumor surgery.
orthopaedic_context.action_required: Add medicine_clinical orthopaedic domain context (ref only — no separate ortho L3).

orthopaedic_context.relevance: Nerve conduction principles essential for peripheral nerve injury, compression syndromes, and EMG interpretation.
orthopaedic_context.action_required: Add medicine_clinical orthopaedic domain context to this existing node in the spine consolidation pass.

orthopaedic_context.relevance: General OA management precedes surgical arthroplasty decisions in hip and knee reconstruction.

orthopaedic_context.relevance: Bone health optimization reduces fragility fracture and periprosthetic fracture risk. Primary home for metabolic bone disease in orthopaedic context (merged from metabolic_bone L3).

orthopaedic_context.relevance: Inflammatory arthritis drives upper extremity deformity, arthroplasty timing, and perioperative biologic management.

orthopaedic_context.relevance: General preoperative assessment extended by orthopaedic-specific optimization (nutrition, smoking, inflammatory arthritis, prehabilitation).

orthopaedic_context.relevance: Regional and general anesthesia choices affect orthopaedic ERAS pathways, multimodal analgesia, and pain control after TJA.

orthopaedic_context.relevance: Surgical wound complications and SSI prevention bundles for arthroplasty, open fractures, and flap coverage.

orthopaedic_context.relevance: VTE is a leading complication after hip/knee arthroplasty and hip fracture surgery with orthopaedic-specific prophylaxis protocols.
orthopaedic_context.action_required: Build L4/L5 orthopaedic VTE prophylaxis context for arthroplasty and hip fracture; include in ABOS and Orthobullets lenses.

orthopaedic_context.relevance: Cellulitis and necrotizing infection overlap with septic arthritis and osteomyelitis workup in orthopaedic practice.
